 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","title":"Implement: incremental patch API for renderer (dirty spans, diff runs)","description":"Goal
- Provide an efficient change representation so renderers can update incrementally.

Requirements
- Dirty tracking at row + span granularity.
- Stable, deterministic patch ordering.
- APIs designed for both WebGPU and native present paths.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchmarks demonstrate sub-linear updates for typical workloads.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","title":"Test: proptest invariants for grid + parser (never panic; stable semantics)","description":"Goal
- Add property tests that make it safe to aggressively optimize and refactor.

Invariants (examples)
- Cursor always within bounds.
- Lines never exceed width in cell count.
- Parser accepts arbitrary bytes without panic.
- Applying a patch preserves semantic equality vs full recompute.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run in CI with fixed seeds + shrinkable counterexamples.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10","title":"FrankenTerm Remote: bidirectional protocol + PTY/websocket bridge","description":"Why
- Replacing xterm.js means we must support the full bidirectional terminal contract:
  - output parsing (VT/ANSI)
  - input encoding (keyboard/mouse -> bytes)
  - terminal replies to application queries (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - transport to a real PTY (local or remote)

Scope
- A minimal but correct PTY bridge (Rust) that can run locally and in production contexts.
- A secure websocket protocol for browser <-> PTY.
- End-to-end demos: connect FrankenTerm.WASM to a real shell and run scripted sessions.

Non-Negotiables
- Deterministic trace logging (JSONL) for every E2E session.
- Security: origin restrictions, explicit allowlists, no arbitrary command execution in prod.

Success Criteria
- Browser terminal can connect to a PTY bridge, run commands, and render accurately.
- Golden transcript tests prevent regressions.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm websocket protocol + threat model","description":"Goal
- Define the browser <-> PTY transport protocol and a production-safe threat model.

Must Cover
- Message types
  - input bytes / semantic events
  - resize
  - output chunks
  - terminal replies (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - clipboard (optional)
  - keepalive
- Versioning + schema
  - protocol version negotiation
  - JSON Schema / binary schema (explicit)
- Backpressure + flow control (prevent OOM)
  - bounded queues
  - coalescing policies (resize storms, mouse move floods)
  - fairness: interactive input must not be starved by output
- Security model
  - origin restrictions, auth, rate limits
  - allowed commands (if any), sandboxing
  - no implicit file access
  - explicit threat model + mitigations
- Capability identity
  - TERM/capability profile story for remote PTY sessions (ties to terminfo bead)
- Logging
  - every session emits JSONL with run_id/session_id, git_sha/build_id, sizes, wire counters, latency histograms

Acceptance Criteria
- Protocol is versioned and has a schema.
- E2E scripts can generate deterministic transcripts for golden checks.
- Threat model includes an explicit loss matrix (false allow vs false block) and a documented decision policy.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","title":"Implement: browser input encoder (DOM events -> terminal byte stream)","description":"Goal
- Encode browser keyboard/mouse events into a VT-compatible input byte stream suitable for a remote PTY.

Scope
- Key encoding: arrows, function keys, modifiers, paste, and common shortcuts.
- Mouse reporting sequences when enabled (SGR mouse).
- Respect feature toggles (bracketed paste, focus events, kitty keyboard where applicable).

Testing
- Unit tests for key/mouse mapping tables.
- Property tests for modifier state machines (no stuck mods).
- JSONL logs for E2E sessions include encoded byte lengths and event counts.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","title":"Implement: terminal reply engine (DSR/DA/DEC queries)","description":"Goal
- Support the bidirectional terminal contract by answering application queries (e.g. cursor position, device attributes).

Scope
- Identify and implement the minimal required set of queries for common CLI apps.
- Replies must be deterministic and capability-profiled.

Testing
- Conformance fixtures: query -> expected reply bytes.
- Differential tests against reference terminals where possible.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.4","title":"Implement: PTY bridge server (Rust) with websocket transport","description":"Goal
- Implement a Rust service that spawns a PTY and bridges I/O over websockets to FrankenTerm.WASM.

Scope
- Use portable-pty (already in tree) or a better alternative if justified.
- Enforce security constraints from the threat model.
- Expose structured telemetry: bytes in/out, latency, resize counts, exit status.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can run locally and drive interactive shells.
- Produces JSONL logs conforming to the shared schema.
